mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE spikes without corresponding SPX declines.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for 60d horizon, or 60d returns in high INDPRO/HOUST regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary policy regime characterized by rising federal funds rates and stagnant CPI inflation leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as bond yields pressure equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL relationship breaks down during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or INDPRO declines correlate positively with SPX returns (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending as measured by real sales (RSAFS) combined with stable inflation indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or RSAFS/CPIAUCSL relationship breaks down during demand shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth exceeding 200k jobs per month signals a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through inflationary pressure and corporate investment optimism.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 35% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with SPX returns (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDP growth following a contraction signals economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved business confidence and consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive after two consecutive quarterly declines
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or GDP growth fails to sustain positive momentum (rolling 3q average < 0).
